metadata.name: SOC 2 Type II
metadata.description: SOC 2 Type II is an auditing framework developed by the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants (AICPA) based on Trust Services Criteria (TSC). It evaluates an organization's information systems relevant to security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy over a period of time (typically 6-12 months). SOC 2 Type II reports provide assurance that controls are not only suitably designed but also operating effectively over the audit period.
metadata.source_organization: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants (AICPA)
metadata.classification_reasoning: SOC 2 is a certification/attestation framework requiring independent CPA firm audit against AICPA Trust Services Criteria, resulting in a formal SOC 2 Type II report with auditor opinion on control effectiveness over time.
metadata.framework_purpose: To provide assurance to stakeholders that a service organization's systems and controls meet AICPA Trust Services Criteria for security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and/or privacy.
metadata.framework_scope: Service organizations that store, process, or transmit customer data, including SaaS providers, cloud service providers, data centers, managed IT service providers, and any organization providing services where customers need assurance over controls.
framework_objectives: Evaluate suitability of design and operating effectiveness of controls
Provide independent assurance over security controls
Demonstrate availability and reliability of systems
Ensure processing integrity of system transactions
Protect confidential information throughout its lifecycle
Safeguard personal information in accordance with privacy commitments
metadata.target_audience: Service Organizations, SaaS Providers, Cloud Service Providers, Data Center Operators, Managed Service Providers, CPA Auditors, Enterprise Customers, Procurement Teams, and Information Security Professionals
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: While SOC 2 is voluntary, lack of a SOC 2 report can result in loss of customer contracts, inability to win enterprise deals, increased audit burden from individual customer audits, and reputational risk.
adoption_approach: Define scope of systems and Trust Services Categories applicable
Perform readiness assessment against Trust Services Criteria
Design and implement controls to address criteria
Operate controls for the audit observation period (6-12 months)
Engage CPA firm to perform Type II examination
Issue SOC 2 Type II report to stakeholders

sections: CC6: Logical and Physical Access Controls

secondary_patterns: Points of Focus within each criterion

controls.title: COSO Principle 1: Commitment to Integrity and Ethical Values
controls.description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values. The board of directors and management, at all levels, demonstrate through directives, actions, and behavior the importance of integrity and ethical values to support the functioning of the system of internal control.
controls.full_text: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values. Points of focus include: (1) Sets the Tone at the Top — The board of directors and management, at all levels, demonstrate through their directives, actions, and behavior the importance of integrity and ethical values; (2) Establishes Standards of Conduct — Expectations of the board of directors and senior management concerning integrity and ethical values are defined in entity standards of conduct and understood at all levels; (3) Evaluates Adherence to Standards of Conduct — Processes are in place to evaluate the performance of individuals and teams against expected standards of conduct; (4) Addresses Deviations in a Timely Manner — Deviations from expected standards are identified and remedied in a timely and consistent manner.
evidence_requirements: Code of conduct/ethics policy
Board of directors meeting minutes demonstrating oversight
Employee acknowledgment records for code of conduct
Disciplinary action records for conduct violations

controls.title: COSO Principle 2: Board Independence and Oversight
controls.description: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.
controls.full_text: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control. Points of focus include: (1) Establishes Oversight Responsibilities — The board identifies and accepts its oversight responsibilities in relation to established requirements and expectations; (2) Applies Relevant Expertise — The board defines, maintains, and periodically evaluates skills and expertise needed among its members; (3) Operates Independently — The board has sufficient members who are independent from management; (4) Provides Oversight for the System of Internal Control — The board retains oversight responsibility for management's design, implementation, and conduct of internal control.
evidence_requirements: Board charter and composition documentation
Board independence declarations
Board meeting minutes showing internal control oversight
Committee structure documentation (audit committee, risk committee)

controls.title: COSO Principle 3: Management Establishes Structure, Authority, and Responsibility
controls.description: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.
controls.full_text: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Considers All Structures of the Entity — Management and the board consider the multiple structures used to support the achievement of objectives; (2) Establishes Reporting Lines — Management designs and evaluates lines of reporting for each entity structure; (3) Defines, Assigns, and Limits Authorities and Responsibilities — Management and the board delegate authority, define responsibilities, and use appropriate processes and technology to assign responsibility and segregate duties.
evidence_requirements: Organizational chart with reporting lines
RACI matrix for key security functions
Job descriptions with defined authorities and responsibilities
Segregation of duties matrix

controls.title: COSO Principle 4: Commitment to Competence
controls.description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Establishes Policies and Practices — Policies and practices reflect expectations of competence necessary to support the achievement of objectives; (2) Evaluates Competence and Addresses Shortcomings — The board and management evaluate competence across the entity in relation to established policies and practices; (3) Attracts, Develops, and Retains Individuals — The entity provides the mentoring, training, and career development needed to attract, develop, and retain sufficient and competent personnel; (4) Plans and Prepares for Succession — Senior management and the board develop contingency plans for key roles.
evidence_requirements: Training program documentation and completion records
Competency assessment records
Hiring and onboarding procedures including background checks
Succession planning documentation for key IT and security roles

controls.title: COSO Principle 5: Accountability for Internal Control
controls.description: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Enforces Accountability Through Structures, Authorities, and Responsibilities — Management and the board establish the mechanisms to communicate and hold individuals accountable for performance of internal control; (2) Establishes Performance Measures, Incentives, and Rewards — Management and the board establish performance measures, incentives, and other rewards appropriate for responsibilities at all levels; (3) Evaluates Performance Measures, Incentives, and Rewards for Ongoing Relevance — Management and the board align incentives and rewards with the fulfillment of internal control responsibilities; (4) Considers Excessive Pressures — Management and the board evaluate and adjust pressures associated with the achievement of objectives.
evidence_requirements: Performance evaluation criteria including security responsibilities
Security KPI dashboards and reports
Accountability framework documentation
Incentive and reward program documentation related to control objectives

controls.title: COSO Principle 13: Quality Information for Internal Control
controls.description: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal control.
controls.full_text: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal control. Points of focus include: (1) Identifies Information Requirements — A process is in place to identify the information required and expected to support the functioning of the other components of internal control and the achievement of the entity's objectives; (2) Captures Internal and External Sources of Data — Information systems capture internal and external sources of data; (3) Processes Relevant Data Into Information — Information systems process and transform relevant data into information; (4) Maintains Quality Throughout Processing — Information systems produce information that is timely, current, accurate, complete, accessible, protected, verifiable, and retained; (5) Considers Costs and Benefits — The nature, quantity, and precision of information communicated are commensurate with and support the achievement of objectives.
evidence_requirements: Information classification policy
Data quality management procedures
System-generated reports demonstrating data integrity
Information flow diagrams for critical processes

controls.title: COSO Principle 14: Internal Communication
controls.description: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.
controls.full_text: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control. Points of focus include: (1) Communicates Internal Control Information — A process is in place to communicate required information to enable all personnel to understand and carry out their internal control responsibilities; (2) Communicates With the Board of Directors — Communication exists between management and the board of directors so that both have information needed to fulfill their roles; (3) Provides Separate Communication Lines — Separate communication channels, such as whistle-blower hotlines, are in place and serve as fail-safe mechanisms to enable anonymous or confidential communication; (4) Selects Relevant Method of Communication — The method of communication considers the timing, audience, and nature of the information.
evidence_requirements: Internal security communication procedures
Security awareness program materials
Whistleblower/anonymous reporting mechanism documentation
Board reporting packages on internal controls and security

controls.title: COSO Principle 15: External Communication
controls.description: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal control.
controls.full_text: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal control. Points of focus include: (1) Communicates to External Parties — Processes are in place to communicate relevant and timely information to external parties including shareholders, partners, owners, regulators, customers, financial analysts, and other external parties; (2) Enables Inbound Communications — Open communication channels allow input from customers, consumers, suppliers, external auditors, regulators, and others; (3) Communicates With the Board of Directors — Relevant information resulting from assessments conducted by external parties is communicated to the board; (4) Provides Separate Communication Lines — Separate communication channels are available to external parties; (5) Selects Relevant Method of Communication — The method of communication considers the timing, audience, and nature of the communication and legal, regulatory, and fiduciary requirements and expectations.
evidence_requirements: Customer communication procedures for security incidents
External audit communication protocols
Service level agreements with security provisions
Public-facing security and privacy disclosures

controls.title: COSO Principle 6: Specifies Suitable Objectives
controls.description: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Operations Objectives — considers tolerances for risk, reflects management choices, includes operations and financial performance goals, and forms a basis for committing of resources; (2) External Financial Reporting Objectives — complies with applicable accounting standards, considers materiality, and reflects entity activities; (3) External Non-Financial Reporting Objectives — complies with applicable law, regulations, and standards established by external standard setters; (4) Internal Reporting Objectives — reflects management choices and the information necessary to support management decisions; (5) Compliance Objectives — reflects applicable laws and regulations.
evidence_requirements: Documented security and operational objectives
Risk tolerance statements
Compliance requirements inventory
Objective-setting methodology documentation

controls.title: COSO Principle 7: Identifies and Analyzes Risk
controls.description: The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives across the entity and analyzes risks as a basis for determining how the risks should be managed.
controls.full_text: The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives across the entity and analyzes risks as a basis for determining how the risks should be managed. Points of focus include: (1) Includes Entity, Subsidiary, Division, Operating Unit, and Functional Levels — The entity identifies and assesses risk at the entity, subsidiary, division, operating unit, and functional levels relevant to the achievement of objectives; (2) Analyzes Internal and External Factors — Risk identification considers internal factors and external factors; (3) Involves Appropriate Levels of Management — The entity puts into place effective risk assessment mechanisms that involve appropriate levels of management; (4) Estimates Significance of Risks Identified — Identified risks are analyzed through a process that includes estimating the potential significance of the risk; (5) Determines How to Respond to Risks — Risk assessment includes considering how the risk should be managed and whether to accept, avoid, reduce, or share the risk.
evidence_requirements: Risk assessment methodology and framework
Risk register with identified risks, likelihood, and impact
Risk treatment plans with risk response decisions
Annual risk assessment reports

controls.title: COSO Principle 8: Considers Potential for Fraud
controls.description: The entity considers the potential for fraud in assessing risks to the achievement of objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity considers the potential for fraud in assessing risks to the achievement of objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Considers Various Types of Fraud — The assessment of fraud considers fraudulent reporting, possible loss of assets, and corruption resulting from the various ways that fraud and misconduct can occur; (2) Assesses Incentives and Pressures — The assessment of fraud risk considers incentives and pressures; (3) Assesses Opportunities — The assessment of fraud risk considers opportunities for unauthorized acquisition, use, or disposal of assets, altering the entity's reporting records, or committing other inappropriate acts; (4) Assesses Attitudes and Rationalizations — The assessment of fraud risk considers how management and other personnel might engage in or justify inappropriate actions.
evidence_requirements: Fraud risk assessment documentation
Anti-fraud controls inventory
Insider threat program documentation
Background check and screening procedures

controls.title: COSO Principle 9: Identifies and Assesses Significant Change
controls.description: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could significantly impact the system of internal control.
controls.full_text: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could significantly impact the system of internal control. Points of focus include: (1) Assesses Changes in the External Environment — The risk identification process considers changes to the regulatory, economic, and physical environment in which the entity operates; (2) Assesses Changes in the Business Model — The entity considers the potential impacts of new business lines, dramatically altered compositions of existing business lines, acquired or divested business operations, and rapid growth; (3) Assesses Changes in Leadership — The entity considers changes in management and respective attitudes and philosophies on the system of internal control.
evidence_requirements: Change management risk assessment process
Environmental scanning and threat intelligence reports
Business model change impact assessments
Management of change procedures for significant organizational changes

controls.title: COSO Principle 16: Selects and Develops Ongoing and Separate Evaluations
controls.description: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning.
controls.full_text: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning. Points of focus include: (1) Considers a Mix of Ongoing and Separate Evaluations — Management includes a balance of ongoing and separate evaluations; (2) Considers Rate of Change — Management considers the rate of change in business and business processes when selecting and developing ongoing and separate evaluations; (3) Establishes Baseline Understanding — The design and current state of an internal control system are used to establish a baseline for ongoing and separate evaluations; (4) Uses Knowledgeable Personnel — Evaluators performing ongoing and separate evaluations have sufficient knowledge to understand what is being evaluated; (5) Integrates With Business Processes — Ongoing evaluations are built into the business processes and adjust to changing conditions; (6) Adjusts Scope and Frequency — Management varies the scope and frequency of separate evaluations depending on risk; (7) Objectively Evaluates — Separate evaluations are performed periodically to provide objective feedback.
evidence_requirements: Internal audit plan and schedule
Continuous monitoring program documentation
Security control testing results and reports
Penetration testing and vulnerability assessment reports

controls.title: COSO Principle 17: Evaluates and Communicates Deficiencies
controls.description: The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those parties responsible for taking corrective action, including senior management and the board of directors, as appropriate.
controls.full_text: The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those parties responsible for taking corrective action, including senior management and the board of directors, as appropriate. Points of focus include: (1) Assesses Results — Management and the board, as appropriate, assess results of ongoing and separate evaluations; (2) Communicates Deficiencies — Deficiencies are communicated to parties responsible for taking corrective action and to senior management and the board of directors, as appropriate; (3) Monitors Corrective Actions — Management tracks whether deficiencies are remedied on a timely basis.
evidence_requirements: Control deficiency tracking and remediation log
Management reporting on control exceptions
Board reporting on significant control deficiencies
Corrective action plans with timelines and status

controls.title: COSO Principle 10: Selects and Develops Control Activities
controls.description: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels.
controls.full_text: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels. Points of focus include: (1) Integrates With Risk Assessment — Control activities help ensure that risk responses that address and mitigate risks are carried out; (2) Considers Entity-Specific Factors — Management considers how the environment, complexity, nature, and scope of its operations, as well as the specific characteristics of its organization, affect the selection and development of control activities; (3) Determines Relevant Business Processes — Management determines which relevant business processes require control activities; (4) Evaluates a Mix of Control Activity Types — Control activities include a range and variety of controls and may include a balance of approaches to mitigate risks; (5) Considers at What Level Activities Are Applied — Management considers control activities at various levels in the entity; (6) Addresses Segregation of Duties — Management segregates incompatible duties, and where such segregation is not practical, management selects and develops alternative control activities.
evidence_requirements: Control activities inventory mapped to risks
Segregation of duties matrix and analysis
Control design documentation
Risk-control mapping matrix

controls.title: COSO Principle 11: Selects and Develops General Controls over Technology
controls.description: The entity also selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity also selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Determines Dependency Between the Use of Technology in Business Processes and Technology General Controls — Management understands and determines the dependency and linkage between business processes, automated control activities, and technology general controls; (2) Establishes Relevant Technology Infrastructure Control Activities — Management selects and develops control activities over the technology infrastructure; (3) Establishes Relevant Security Management Process Control Activities — Management selects and develops control activities that are designed and implemented to restrict technology access rights to authorized users; (4) Establishes Relevant Technology Acquisition, Development, and Maintenance Process Control Activities — Management selects and develops control activities over the acquisition, development, and maintenance of technology and its infrastructure.
evidence_requirements: IT General Controls (ITGC) framework documentation
Technology infrastructure control inventory
Security management process documentation
Technology acquisition and development lifecycle controls

controls.title: COSO Principle 12: Deploys Through Policies and Procedures
controls.description: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and in procedures that put policies into action.
controls.full_text: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and in procedures that put policies into action. Points of focus include: (1) Establishes Policies and Procedures to Support Deployment of Management's Directives — Management establishes control activities that are built into business processes and employees' day-to-day activities through policies establishing what is expected and relevant procedures specifying actions; (2) Establishes Responsibility and Accountability for Executing Policies and Procedures — Management establishes responsibility and accountability for control activities with management (or other designated personnel) of the business unit or function in which the relevant risks reside; (3) Performs in a Timely Manner — Responsible personnel perform control activities in a timely manner as defined by the policies and procedures; (4) Takes Corrective Action — Responsible personnel investigate and act on matters identified as a result of executing control activities; (5) Performs Using Competent Personnel — Competent personnel with sufficient authority perform control activities with diligence and continuing focus; (6) Reassesses Policies and Procedures — Management periodically reviews control activities to determine their continued relevance.
evidence_requirements: Information security policy library
Standard operating procedures for key controls
Policy review and approval records
Policy exception management process

controls.title: Logical and Physical Access Security Software, Infrastructure, and Architectures
controls.description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Identifies and Manages the Inventory of Information Assets — The entity identifies, inventories, classifies, and manages information assets; (2) Restricts Logical Access — Logical access to information assets, including hardware, data (at-rest, during processing, or in transmission), software, administrative authorities, mobile devices, output, and offline system components is restricted through the use of access control software and rule sets; (3) Identifies and Authenticates Users — Persons, infrastructure, and software are identified and authenticated prior to accessing information assets; (4) Considers Network Segmentation — Network segmentation permits unrelated portions of the entity's information system to be isolated from each other; (5) Manages Points of Access — Points of access by outside entities and the types of data that flow through the points of access are identified, inventoried, and managed; (6) Restricts Access to Information Assets — Combinations of data classification, separate data structures, port restrictions, access protocol restrictions, user identification, and digital certificates are used to establish access control rules for information assets; (7) Manages Identification and Authentication — Identification and authentication requirements are established, documented, and managed; (8) Manages Credentials for Infrastructure and Software — New internal and external infrastructure and software are registered, authorized, and documented prior to being granted access credentials and implemented on the network or access point.
evidence_requirements: Information asset inventory and classification
Access control policy and procedures
Network architecture and segmentation diagrams
Identity and access management system configuration

controls.title: User Access Provisioning and De-provisioning
controls.description: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity.
controls.full_text: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity. For those users whose access is administered by the entity, user system credentials are removed when user access is no longer authorized. Points of focus include: (1) Controls Access Credentials to Protected Assets — Information asset access credentials are created based on an authorization from the system's asset owner or authorized custodian; (2) Removes Access to Protected Assets When Appropriate — Processes are in place to remove credential access when an individual no longer requires such access; (3) Reviews Appropriateness of Access Credentials — The appropriateness of access credentials is reviewed on a periodic basis for unnecessary and inappropriate individuals with credentials.
evidence_requirements: User access provisioning procedures and approval records
User access de-provisioning/termination procedures and evidence
Periodic user access reviews (at least quarterly)
Privileged access management procedures

controls.title: Role-Based Access and Least Privilege
controls.description: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties.
controls.full_text: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties, to meet the entity's objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Creates or Modifies Access to Protected Information Assets — Processes are in place to create or modify access to protected information assets based on authorization from the asset's owner; (2) Removes Access to Protected Information Assets — Processes are in place to remove access to protected information assets when an individual no longer requires access; (3) Uses Role-Based Access Controls — Role-based access control is used to support segregation of incompatible functions; (4) Reviews Access Roles and Rules — The appropriateness of access roles and access rules is reviewed on a periodic basis.
evidence_requirements: Role-based access control (RBAC) configuration documentation
Least privilege implementation evidence
Access modification request and approval records
Periodic access role review documentation

controls.title: Physical Access Restrictions
controls.description: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets (for example, data center facilities, back-up media storage, and other sensitive locations) to authorized personnel to meet the entity's objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets (for example, data center facilities, back-up media storage, and other sensitive locations) to authorized personnel to meet the entity's objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Creates or Modifies Physical Access — Processes are in place to create or modify physical access to facilities such as data centers, office spaces, and work areas, based on authorization from the system's asset owner; (2) Removes Physical Access — Processes are in place to remove physical access to facilities when an individual no longer requires access; (3) Reviews Physical Access — Physical access to facilities is reviewed periodically to ensure consistency with job responsibilities.
evidence_requirements: Physical access control system documentation
Data center access logs and reviews
Visitor management procedures and logs
Physical access provisioning and de-provisioning records

controls.title: Logical and Physical Access Disposal and Destruction
controls.description: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished and is no longer required to meet the entity's objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished and is no longer required to meet the entity's objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Identifies Data and Software for Disposal — Procedures are in place to identify data and software stored on equipment to be disposed and to render such data and software unreadable; (2) Removes Data and Software From Entity Control — Procedures are in place to remove data and software stored on equipment before it is moved from the entity's physical control.
evidence_requirements: Media sanitization and disposal policy
Asset disposal and destruction records with certificates
Data wiping/destruction verification evidence
Hardware decommissioning procedures

controls.title: Security Measures Against Threats Outside System Boundaries
controls.description: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
controls.full_text: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries. Points of focus include: (1) Restricts Access — The types of activities that can occur through a communication channel (for example, FTP site, router port) are restricted; (2) Protects Identification and Authentication Credentials — Identification and authentication credentials are protected during transmission outside its system boundaries; (3) Requires Additional Authentication or Credentials — Additional authentication information or credentials are required when accessing the system from outside its boundaries; (4) Implements Boundary Protection Systems — Points of access by outside entities and the types of data that flow through the points of access are identified, inventoried, and managed.
evidence_requirements: Firewall and network security control configurations
Intrusion detection/prevention system (IDS/IPS) configurations
VPN and remote access security configurations
Multi-factor authentication implementation for external access

controls.title: Restricts Data Transmission, Movement, and Removal
controls.description: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity's objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity's objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Restricts the Ability to Perform Transmission — Data loss prevention processes and technologies are used to restrict ability to authorize and execute the transmission, movement, and removal of information; (2) Uses Encryption Technologies or Secure Communication Channels to Protect Data — Encryption technologies or secured communication channels are used to protect data during transmission; (3) Protects Removal Media — Encryption technologies and physical asset protections are used to protect data stored on media; (4) Protects Mobile Devices — Processes are in place to protect data on mobile devices.
evidence_requirements: Data loss prevention (DLP) tool configuration and reports
Encryption-in-transit configurations (TLS/SSL)
Removable media policy and encryption controls
Mobile device management (MDM) policy and configuration

controls.title: Prevention and Detection of Unauthorized Software
controls.description: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software to meet the entity's objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software to meet the entity's objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Restricts Application and Software Installation — The ability to install applications and software is restricted to authorized individuals; (2) Detects Unauthorized Changes to Software and Configuration Parameters — Processes are in place to detect changes to software and configuration parameters that may be indicative of unauthorized or malicious software; (3) Uses a Defined Change Control Process — A management-defined change control process is used for the implementation of software; (4) Uses Antivirus and Anti-Malware Software — Antivirus and anti-malware software is implemented and maintained to provide for the interception or detection and remediation of malware; (5) Scans Information Assets from Outside the Entity for Malware and Other Unauthorized Software — Procedures are in place to scan information assets that have been transferred or returned to the entity's custody for malware and other unauthorized software.
evidence_requirements: Anti-malware/endpoint protection deployment evidence
Software installation restriction policies and controls
File integrity monitoring (FIM) configuration and alerts
Application whitelisting/control documentation

controls.title: Detection and Monitoring of Security Events
controls.description: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify (1) changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities, and (2) susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.
controls.full_text: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify (1) changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities, and (2) susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities. Points of focus include: (1) Uses Defined Configuration Standards — Management has defined configuration standards; (2) Monitors Infrastructure and Software — The entity monitors infrastructure and software for noncompliance with the standards, which could threaten the achievement of the entity's objectives; (3) Implements Change-Detection Mechanisms — The entity implements change-detection mechanisms to manage unauthorized changes to software; (4) Detects Unknown or Unauthorized Components — Procedures are in place to detect the introduction of unknown or unauthorized components; (5) Conducts Vulnerability Scans — The entity conducts vulnerability scans designed to identify potential vulnerabilities or misconfigurations on a periodic basis and after significant changes.
evidence_requirements: Vulnerability scanning schedules, tools, and reports
Configuration monitoring and baseline comparison reports
Security information and event management (SIEM) configuration
Infrastructure monitoring tool configurations and alert rules

controls.title: Monitoring System Components for Anomalies
controls.description: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events. Points of focus include: (1) Implements Detection Policies, Procedures, and Tools — Detection policies and procedures are defined and implemented and detection tools are implemented on infrastructure and software to identify anomalies in the operation or unusual activity on systems; (2) Designs Detection Measures — Detection measures are designed to identify anomalies that could result from actual or attempted security events, including impairment to the availability and integrity of the system; (3) Implements Filters to Analyze Anomalies — Management has established criteria to filter, summarize, and report anomalies; (4) Monitors Detection Tools for Effective Operation — Management has implemented processes to monitor the effectiveness of detection tools.
evidence_requirements: SIEM log monitoring and alerting evidence
Anomaly detection tool configurations and tuning records
Security operations center (SOC) procedures
Alert triage and escalation procedures

controls.title: Security Event Evaluation
controls.description: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures.
controls.full_text: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures. Points of focus include: (1) Responds to Security Incidents — Procedures are in place for responding to security incidents and evaluating the effectiveness of those procedures on a periodic basis; (2) Communicates and Reviews Detected Security Events — Detected security events are communicated to and reviewed by the individuals responsible for the management of the security program and actions are taken, if necessary; (3) Develops and Implements Procedures to Analyze Security Incidents — Procedures are in place to analyze security incidents and determine system impact; (4) Assesses the Impact on Personal Information — Processes are in place to assess the impact of security incidents on personal information.
evidence_requirements: Incident response plan and procedures
Security incident classification and prioritization criteria
Incident investigation and analysis reports
Post-incident review and lessons learned documentation

controls.title: Security Incident Response
controls.description: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.
controls.full_text: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate. Points of focus include: (1) Assigns Roles and Responsibilities — Roles and responsibilities for the design, implementation, maintenance, and execution of the incident response program are assigned; (2) Contains Security Incidents — Procedures are in place to contain security incidents that actively threaten entity objectives; (3) Mitigates Ongoing Security Incidents — Procedures are in place to mitigate the effects of ongoing security incidents; (4) Ends Threats Posed by Security Incidents — Procedures are in place to end the threats posed by security incidents through closure of the vulnerability, removal of unauthorized access, and other remediation actions; (5) Restores Operations — Procedures are in place to restore data and business operations to an acceptable level following a security incident; (6) Develops and Implements Communication Protocols for Security Incidents — Protocols for communicating security incidents and actions taken to affected parties are developed and implemented; (7) Obtains Understanding of Nature of Incident and Determines Containment Strategy — An understanding of the nature of the incident, the affected system components, and the containment strategy is obtained; (8) Remediates Identified Vulnerabilities — Identified vulnerabilities related to the incident are remediated to prevent further incidents; (9) Communicates Remediation Activities — Remediation activities are documented and communicated.
evidence_requirements: Incident response plan with defined roles and escalation procedures
Incident response team roster and contact information
Incident response tabletop exercise records
Security incident communication templates and notification records

controls.title: Recovery from Security Incidents
controls.description: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.
controls.full_text: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents. Points of focus include: (1) Restores the Affected Environment — The activities restore the affected environment to functional operation by rebuilding systems, updating software, installing patches, and changing configurations, as needed; (2) Communicates Information About the Event — Communications about the nature of the incident, recovery actions taken, and activities required for the prevention of future security events are made to management and others as appropriate; (3) Determines Root Cause of the Event — The root cause of the event is determined; (4) Implements Changes to Prevent and Detect Recurrences — Additional architecture, infrastructure, and operating changes are implemented to prevent and detect recurrences on a timely basis; (5) Improves Response and Recovery Procedures — Lessons learned are analyzed and the incident response plan and recovery procedures are improved.
evidence_requirements: Disaster recovery and business continuity plans
Root cause analysis documentation
Recovery testing and validation records
Post-incident improvement action items and tracking

controls.title: Change Management Controls
controls.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Manages Changes Throughout the System Life Cycle — A process for managing system changes throughout the life cycle of the system and its components is used to support system availability and processing integrity; (2) Authorizes Changes — A process is in place to authorize system changes prior to development; (3) Designs and Develops Changes — A process is in place to design and develop system changes; (4) Documents Changes — A process is in place to document system changes to support ongoing maintenance of the system and to support system users in performing their responsibilities; (5) Tracks System Changes — A process is in place to track system changes prior to implementation; (6) Configures Software — A process is in place to select and implement the configuration parameters used to control the functionality of software; (7) Tests System Changes — A process is in place to test system changes prior to implementation in the production environment; (8) Approves System Changes — A process is in place to approve system changes prior to implementation; (9) Deploys System Changes — A process is in place to implement system changes; (10) Identifies and Evaluates System Changes — Objectives affected by system changes are identified and the ability of the modified system to meet the objectives is evaluated throughout the system development life cycle; (11) Identifies Changes in Infrastructure, Data, Software, and Procedures Required to Remediate Incidents — Changes in infrastructure, data, software, and procedures required to remediate incidents are identified and the change process is initiated upon identification; (12) Creates Baseline Configuration of IT Technology — A baseline configuration of IT technology is created and maintained; (13) Provides for Changes Necessary in Emergency Situations — A process is in place for authorizing, designing, testing, approving, and implementing changes necessary in emergency situations.
evidence_requirements: Change management policy and procedures
Change request and approval records (CAB meeting minutes)
Change testing and validation documentation
Emergency change procedures and post-implementation reviews

controls.title: Risk Mitigation Identification and Assessment
controls.description: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.
controls.full_text: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions. Points of focus include: (1) Considers Mitigation of Risks of Business Disruption — Risk mitigation activities include the development of planned policies, procedures, communications, and alternative processing solutions to respond to, mitigate, and recover from security events that disrupt business operations; (2) Considers the Use of Insurance to Mitigate Financial Impact Risks — The entity considers the use of insurance to mitigate the financial impact of loss events that would otherwise impair its ability to meet its objectives.
evidence_requirements: Business disruption risk assessment and mitigation plans
Business continuity and disaster recovery plans
Cyber insurance policy documentation
Risk treatment plans for identified business disruption risks

controls.title: Vendor and Business Partner Risk Management
controls.description: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners.
controls.full_text: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners. Points of focus include: (1) Establishes Requirements for Vendor and Business Partner Engagements — Requirements for vendor and business partner engagements are established based on the vendor or business partner's criticality to the entity and the entity's risk tolerance; (2) Assesses Vendor and Business Partner Risks — Risks are assessed when establishing or significantly changing relationships with vendors and business partners; (3) Assigns Responsibility and Accountability for Managing Vendors and Business Partners — Responsibility and accountability for managing vendors and business partners are assigned; (4) Establishes Communication Protocols for Vendors and Business Partners — Communication protocols with vendors and business partners are established; (5) Establishes Exception Handling Procedures From Vendor and Business Partners — Exception handling procedures from vendor and business partner requirements are established; (6) Assesses Vendor and Business Partner Performance — Vendor and business partner performance is periodically assessed; (7) Implements Procedures for Addressing Issues Identified During Vendor and Business Partner Assessments — Procedures are in place to address issues identified during assessments of vendor and business partner performance; (8) Implements Procedures for Terminating Vendor and Business Partner Relationships — Procedures are in place for terminating vendor and business partner relationships; (9) Obtains Complementary User Entity Controls (CUECs) and Complementary Subservice Organization Controls (CSOCs) — The entity obtains and reviews complementary user entity controls and complementary subservice organization controls.
evidence_requirements: Vendor risk management policy and procedures
Vendor risk assessment questionnaires and results
Vendor contracts with security requirements and SLAs
Annual vendor performance review documentation

controls.title: System Availability Commitments and Requirements
controls.description: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components (infrastructure, data, and software) to manage capacity demand and to enable the implementation of additional capacity to help meet its objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components (infrastructure, data, and software) to manage capacity demand and to enable the implementation of additional capacity to help meet its objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Manages Capacity to Meet Commitments — Sufficient capacity is maintained to achieve commitments for system availability and performance; (2) Monitors Processing Capacity — The use of system components is monitored to manage capacity demand and to enable additional capacity when needed; (3) Manages Prioritization of Resource Usage — When multiple systems are dependent on common resources, prioritization of resource usage is implemented to reduce the risk to one or more systems.
evidence_requirements: Capacity planning documentation and forecasts
Infrastructure monitoring dashboards and alert configurations
Performance metrics and SLA compliance reports
Auto-scaling or capacity expansion procedures

controls.title: Environmental Protections, Software, Data Backup, and Recovery
controls.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data back-up processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data back-up processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Identifies Environmental Threats — Threats that could impair system availability are identified, including threats resulting from adverse weather conditions, failure of environmental control systems, electrical discharge, fire, and flood; (2) Designs Detection Measures — Detection measures are implemented to identify anomalies that could result from environmental threat events; (3) Implements and Maintains Environmental Protection Mechanisms — Environmental protection mechanisms, software, and data recovery processes are implemented and maintained; (4) Tests Recovery Plan Procedures — Recovery plan procedures are tested on a periodic basis; (5) Recovers Using Backups — Recovery plans include procedures to restore system availability and data from backups.
evidence_requirements: Backup policy, schedule, and successful completion logs
Disaster recovery plan and procedures
DR/backup restoration test results and reports
Environmental monitoring system configurations (temperature, humidity, fire suppression)

controls.title: Recovery Plan Testing
controls.description: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Implements Business Continuity Plan Testing — Business continuity plan testing is performed on a periodic basis. The testing includes scenarios of significant business disruptions, such as natural disasters; (2) Tests Integrity and Completeness of Backup Data — The integrity and completeness of backup data is tested on a periodic basis.
evidence_requirements: BCP/DR test plans, scenarios, and schedules
BCP/DR test execution results and findings
Backup integrity and restoration validation records
Post-test improvement action items and tracking

controls.title: Processing Integrity Definitions and Commitments
controls.description: The entity obtains or generates, uses, and communicates relevant, quality information regarding the objectives related to processing, including definitions of data processed and product or service specifications, to support the use of products or services.
controls.full_text: The entity obtains or generates, uses, and communicates relevant, quality information regarding the objectives related to processing, including definitions of data processed and product or service specifications, to support the use of products or services. Points of focus include: (1) Defines Processing Specifications — Processing specifications, including inputs, processing actions, and outputs, are established and documented; (2) Defines Processing Accuracy and Completeness — Processing accuracy and completeness requirements are established and documented; (3) Implements Monitoring of Processing — The entity monitors processing of transactions against processing specifications.
evidence_requirements: Data processing specifications and documentation
Input validation and output reconciliation procedures
Processing accuracy monitoring reports
Data quality metrics and dashboards

controls.title: Processing Completeness, Accuracy, Timeliness, and Authorization
controls.description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs, including controls over completeness, accuracy, timeliness, and authorization to meet the entity's objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs, including controls over completeness, accuracy, timeliness, and authorization to meet the entity's objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Defines Inputs — System inputs are defined and documented; (2) Validates Inputs — Inputs are validated for completeness, accuracy, and timeliness before they are processed; (3) Rejects and Reports Errors — System errors are rejected and reported for correction in a timely manner; (4) Defines Processing — Processing operations are defined and documented; (5) Performs Processing — Processing is performed in accordance with specifications; (6) Defines Outputs — System outputs are defined and documented; (7) Validates Outputs — System output accuracy and completeness are verified.
evidence_requirements: Input validation controls and error handling documentation
Processing reconciliation reports (input-to-output)
Transaction authorization procedures and logs
Error correction and reprocessing procedures

controls.title: Processing Error Identification and Correction
controls.description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system processing to enable the detection, correction, and prevention of processing errors to meet the entity's objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity implements policies and procedures over system processing to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's specified processing integrity objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Detects Processing Errors — Detection mechanisms are in place to detect processing errors; (2) Corrects Processing Errors — Processing errors are corrected in a timely manner; (3) Prevents Processing Errors — Preventive controls are implemented to reduce the occurrence of processing errors.
evidence_requirements: Error detection and exception monitoring reports
Error correction and resubmission procedures
Root cause analysis for recurring processing errors
Automated validation and quality check configurations

controls.title: Output Completeness, Accuracy, and Distribution
controls.description: The entity implements policies and procedures to make available or deliver output completely, accurately, and timely in accordance with specifications to meet the entity's objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity implements policies and procedures to make available or deliver output completely, accurately, and timely in accordance with specifications to meet the entity's objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Protects Output — Output is protected when stored or delivered, or both, to prevent theft, destruction, corruption, or deterioration that would prevent output from meeting specifications; (2) Distributes Output Only to Intended Parties — Output is distributed or made available only to intended parties; (3) Distributes Output Completely and Accurately — Procedures are in place to ensure the completeness, accuracy, and timeliness of distributed output; (4) Creates and Maintains Records of System Output Activities — Records of system output activities are created and maintained.
evidence_requirements: Output delivery verification and acknowledgment logs
Output distribution access controls
Report generation completeness and accuracy validation
Output archival and retention procedures

controls.title: Storage Input and Output Integrity
controls.description: The entity implements policies and procedures to store inputs, items in processing, and outputs completely, accurately, and timely in accordance with system specifications to meet the entity's objectives.
controls.full_text: The entity implements policies and procedures to store inputs, items in processing, and outputs completely, accurately, and timely in accordance with system specifications to meet the entity's objectives. Points of focus include: (1) Protects Stored Items — Stored items are protected to prevent theft, corruption, destruction, or deterioration that would prevent output from meeting specifications; (2) Archives and Protects System Records — System records are archived and protected against theft, corruption, destruction, or deterioration that would prevent them from being used.
evidence_requirements: Data storage integrity controls and checksums
Database integrity monitoring configurations
Storage access control configurations
Data archival and retention procedures

controls.title: Identification and Maintenance of Confidential Information
controls.description: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
controls.full_text: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality. Points of focus include: (1) Identifies Confidential Information — Procedures are in place to identify and designate confidential information when it is received or created and to determine the period over which the confidential information is to be retained; (2) Describes Handling of Confidential Information — Procedures for the handling of confidential information are defined and implemented.
evidence_requirements: Data classification policy and procedures
Confidential information inventory/register
Data handling and labeling procedures
Non-disclosure agreements (NDAs) for employees and contractors

controls.title: Disposal of Confidential Information
controls.description: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
controls.full_text: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality. Points of focus include: (1) Identifies Confidential Information to be Disposed — Procedures are in place to identify confidential information requiring disposal; (2) Disposes of Confidential Information — Procedures are in place to erase or otherwise destroy confidential information that has been identified for disposal.
evidence_requirements: Data retention and disposal schedule
Secure data destruction procedures and certificates
Media sanitization records
Disposal verification and audit trail

controls.title: Privacy Notice
controls.description: The entity provides notice to data subjects about its privacy practices to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy.
controls.full_text: The entity provides notice to data subjects about its privacy practices to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. The notice is updated and communicated to data subjects in a timely manner for changes to the entity's privacy practices, including changes in the use of personal information, to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Provides Notice to Data Subjects — Notice is provided to data subjects regarding the purpose for collecting personal information, the types of personal information collected, how such information is collected, how it is used, retained, disclosed, and disposed of, and the choices available to data subjects; (2) Covers Entities and Activities in Notice — An objective description of the entities and activities covered in the privacy notice is included; (3) Uses Clear and Conspicuous Language — The privacy notice is clear, conspicuous, and easily accessible.
evidence_requirements: Privacy notice/policy published on website and applications
Privacy notice update and version history records
Evidence of notice communication to data subjects
Privacy notice accessibility and language review records

controls.title: Choice and Consent
controls.description: The entity communicates choices available regarding the collection, use, retention, disclosure, and disposal of personal information to the data subjects and obtains implicit or explicit consent with respect to such choices.
controls.full_text: The entity communicates choices available regarding the collection, use, retention, disclosure, and disposal of personal information to the data subjects and the consequences, if any, of each choice to the data subject and obtains implicit or explicit consent with respect to such choices to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Communicates to Data Subjects — Data subjects are informed about the choices available to them with respect to the collection, use, and disclosure of personal information; (2) Communicates Consequences of Withdrawing Consent — Data subjects are informed of the consequences of their privacy choices; (3) Obtains Implicit or Explicit Consent — Implicit or explicit consent is obtained from data subjects at or before the time personal information is collected; (4) Documents and Manages Consent — Consent decisions of data subjects are documented and managed.
evidence_requirements: Consent management platform and configuration
Consent collection records and audit trail
Opt-in/opt-out mechanism documentation
Records of consent withdrawal requests and processing

controls.title: Collection of Personal Information
controls.description: Personal information is collected consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
controls.full_text: Personal information is collected consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Limits the Collection of Personal Information — The collection of personal information is limited to that identified in the notice; (2) Collects Information by Fair and Lawful Means — Methods of collecting personal information are reviewed by management before they are implemented to confirm that personal information is obtained fairly, without intimidation or deception, and lawfully; (3) Collects Information From Reliable Sources — Management confirms that third parties from whom personal information is collected are reliable sources.
evidence_requirements: Data collection inventory mapped to privacy notice purposes
Data minimization assessment documentation
Third-party data source agreements and reliability assessments
Collection method review and approval records

controls.title: Consent for New Purposes or Uses
controls.description: For information requiring explicit consent, the entity communicates the need for such consent, as well as the consequences of a failure to provide consent for the request for personal information, and obtains the consent prior to the collection of the information.
controls.full_text: For information requiring explicit consent, the entity communicates the need for such consent, as well as the consequences of a failure to provide consent for the request for personal information, and obtains the consent prior to the collection of the information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Communicates Need for Explicit Consent — Data subjects are informed about the requirement for explicit consent for specific types of personal information collection; (2) Obtains Explicit Consent Before Collection — Explicit consent is obtained before collecting sensitive or specifically regulated categories of personal information.
evidence_requirements: Explicit consent forms and mechanisms for sensitive data
Records of explicit consent obtained before collection
Communication records informing data subjects of consent requirements
Process for managing consent for new data uses

controls.title: Use, Retention, and Disposal of Personal Information
controls.description: The entity limits the use of personal information to the purposes identified in the entity's objectives related to privacy.
controls.full_text: The entity limits the use of personal information to the purposes identified in the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Uses Personal Information for Identified Purposes — Personal information is used only for the purposes identified in the privacy notice, including purposes compatible with those identified purposes; (2) Retains Personal Information — Personal information retention is limited to the duration needed to fulfill the stated purpose or as required by law; (3) Disposes of Personal Information — Procedures are in place to dispose of personal information when it is no longer needed for the stated purpose or as required by law.
evidence_requirements: Purpose limitation policy and enforcement documentation
Personal information retention schedule
Data disposal and destruction procedures and evidence
Data use monitoring and audit reports

controls.title: Retention and Minimization of Personal Information
controls.description: The entity retains personal information consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy, and minimizes personal information held.
controls.full_text: The entity retains personal information consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Defines Retention Period for Personal Information — Policies define the retention period for personal information based on the purposes identified in the entity's privacy notice and legal requirements; (2) Retains Personal Information Until Purposes Are Met — Personal information is retained until the purposes for which it was collected are met or consent is withdrawn, unless required by law; (3) Minimizes Personal Information Retained — The entity minimizes the amount of personal information retained to what is necessary.
evidence_requirements: Data retention policy with defined periods per data type
Data minimization reviews and assessments
Automated data purge/archival procedures and logs
Periodic review of retained personal information

controls.title: Disposal Methods for Personal Information
controls.description: The entity securely disposes of personal information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy.
controls.full_text: The entity securely disposes of personal information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Captures Personal Information for Disposal — Personal information no longer retained is identified for disposal; (2) Disposes of Personal Information — Personal information identified for disposal is disposed of in a manner that prevents loss, theft, misuse, or unauthorized access.
evidence_requirements: Secure disposal procedures for personal information
Disposal verification records and certificates
Electronic data destruction tool configurations
Physical document shredding procedures and records

controls.title: Data Subject Access
controls.description: The entity grants identified and authenticated data subjects the ability to access their stored personal information for review and, upon request, provides physical or electronic copies of that information to data subjects.
controls.full_text: The entity grants identified and authenticated data subjects the ability to access their stored personal information for review and, upon request, provides physical or electronic copies of that information to data subjects to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Authenticates Data Subjects' Identity — The identity of data subjects who request access to their personal information is authenticated before they are provided with that information; (2) Permits Data Subjects to Access Their Personal Information — Data subjects are able to determine whether the entity maintains personal information about them and, upon request, may obtain access to their personal information; (3) Provides Understandable Personal Information Within Reasonable Time — Personal information is provided to the data subject in an understandable form and in a reasonable time frame.
evidence_requirements: Data subject access request (DSAR) procedures
Identity verification process for data subjects
DSAR tracking and response time metrics
Sample DSAR responses demonstrating completeness

controls.title: Correction of Personal Information
controls.description: The entity corrects, amends, or appends personal information based on information provided by data subjects and communicates such information to third parties.
controls.full_text: The entity corrects, amends, or appends personal information based on information provided by data subjects and communicates such information to third parties, as committed or required, to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Communicates Denial of Access Requests — Data subjects are informed, in writing, about the reason a request for access to their personal information was denied, and the ability to challenge such denial; (2) Permits Data Subjects to Update or Correct Personal Information — Data subjects are able to update or correct personal information held by the entity; (3) Communicates Corrections to Third Parties — Corrections to personal information are communicated to third parties who received the information.
evidence_requirements: Data correction and amendment request procedures
Records of corrections made to personal information
Third-party notification procedures for data corrections
Appeal process documentation for denied access requests

controls.title: Disclosure to Third Parties
controls.description: The entity discloses personal information to third parties with the explicit consent of data subjects, and such disclosure is consistent with the objectives of the entity related to privacy.
controls.full_text: The entity discloses personal information to third parties with the explicit consent of data subjects, and such disclosure is consistent with the objectives of the entity related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Communicates Privacy Policies to Third Parties — Privacy policies or agreements are communicated to third parties to whom personal information is disclosed; (2) Discloses Personal Information Only When Appropriate — Personal information is disclosed to third parties only for the purposes for which it was collected or created and only when implicit or explicit consent has been obtained; (3) Discloses Personal Information Only to Identified Third Parties — Personal information is disclosed only to third parties who have agreements with the entity to protect personal information in a manner consistent with the relevant aspects of the entity's privacy notice or other specific instructions or requirements.
evidence_requirements: Third-party data sharing agreements and privacy addenda
Third-party due diligence assessment records
Data disclosure logs with purpose and consent basis
Third-party privacy compliance monitoring reports

controls.title: Authorized Disclosures and Data Subject Notification
controls.description: The entity creates and retains a complete, accurate, and timely record of authorized disclosures of personal information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy.
controls.full_text: The entity creates and retains a complete, accurate, and timely record of authorized disclosures of personal information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Records Authorized Disclosures — A record of authorized disclosures of personal information is maintained; (2) Creates Record of Detection of Unauthorized Disclosures — Procedures exist for the detection of unauthorized disclosures of personal information; (3) Notifies Affected Data Subjects — Data subjects affected by unauthorized disclosures are notified.
evidence_requirements: Disclosure log/register of personal information sharing
Unauthorized disclosure detection procedures
Breach notification procedures and templates
Records of data subject notifications for unauthorized disclosures

controls.title: Unauthorized Disclosure Notification
controls.description: The entity provides notification of breaches and incidents to affected data subjects, regulators, and others to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy.
controls.full_text: The entity provides notification of breaches and incidents to affected data subjects, regulators, and others to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Remediates Misuse of Personal Information by a Third Party — The entity obtains commitments from vendors and other third parties with access to personal information to notify the entity in the event of actual or suspected unauthorized disclosures of personal information; (2) Reports Actual or Suspected Unauthorized Disclosures — Procedures for the reporting of actual or suspected breaches are established and implemented; (3) Notifies Data Subjects — Data subjects affected by unauthorized disclosures of their personal information are notified in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.
evidence_requirements: Breach notification policy with regulatory timelines
Incident communication templates for data subjects and regulators
Third-party breach notification clauses in contracts
Breach notification tracking and compliance records

controls.title: Disclosure Purposes and Limitations
controls.description: The entity restricts the disclosure of personal information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy.
controls.full_text: The entity restricts the disclosure of personal information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. Points of focus include: (1) Restricts Disclosure to Identified Purposes — Disclosure of personal information is restricted to the purposes for which the personal information was collected and identified in the privacy notice; (2) Restricts Disclosures — Personal information is disclosed only to those parties identified in the privacy notice.
evidence_requirements: Disclosure limitation policies aligned to privacy notice
Data flow mapping showing disclosure recipients
Approval process for new disclosure purposes
Periodic review of disclosure practices

controls.title: Quality of Personal Information
controls.description: The entity collects and maintains accurate, up-to-date, complete, and relevant personal information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy.
